data.label: GS
data.outsideLabel: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands

sql.sqlStatement: Specify the workbook containing the data
SET DATA FILE
Create a node for each country

SELECT   [ISO]                as [Item],
         [ISO]                as [Label],
         [Country]            as [External Label],
         [Continent]          as [Style Name]
FROM     [countries$]
WHERE    [ISO] IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY [Country] ASC
Create a node for each continent

SELECT DISTINCT [Continent]   as [Item],
                [Continent]   as [Label],
                "Continent"   as [Style Name]
FROM     [countries$]
ORDER BY [Continent] ASC
Create an edge between neighboring countries

SELECT   [ISO]                as [Item],
         [Neighbors]          as [Related Item]
FROM     [countries$]
WHERE    [Neighbors] IS NOT NULL
Create an edge between each country and its parent continent

SELECT   [Continent]          as [Item],
         [ISO]                as [Related Item],
         "ContinentToCountry" as [Style Name]
FROM     [countries$]
WHERE    [ISO] IS NOT NULL
Quick Help
Excel SQL Syntax
https://github.com/jjlong150/AceSqlForExcel
Clear the 'data' worksheet
RESET
Specify the workbook containing the data
SET DATA FILE
Create the edges

SELECT [From Column] AS [Item], 
       [To Column]   AS [Related Item] 
FROM   [Worksheet Name$]
Create the nodes

SELECT [Node Id Column]    AS [Item],
       [Node Label Column] AS [Label],
       [Node Style Column] AS [Style Name]
FROM   [Worksheet Name$] 
WHERE  ...
Create edges which chain a set of nodes

SELECT [Node Id Column] AS [ITEM], 
       TRUE             AS [CREATE EDGES]
FROM   [Worksheet Name$]
WHERE  ...
Create a subgraph, with constraints set to rank specified as either 'same' | 'min' | 'source' | 'max' | 'sink'.

Rank constraints on the nodes in a subgraph. If rank="same", all nodes are placed on the same rank. If rank="min", all nodes are placed on the minimum rank. If rank="source", all nodes are placed on the minimum rank, and the only nodes on the minimum rank belong to some subgraph whose rank attribute is "source" or "min". Analogous criteria hold for rank="max" and rank="sink". (Note: the minimum rank is topmost or leftmost, and the maximum rank is bottommost or rightmost.)

SELECT [Node Id Column] AS [ITEM], 
       TRUE             AS [CREATE RANK],
       'same'           AS [RANK]
FROM   [Worksheet Name$]
WHERE  ...
Split text for labels at first blank after 10 characters

SELECT [Label Column] AS [Label],
       10             AS [SPLIT LENGTH],
       ...
Specify the line ending (\l, \n, \r, | etc.) to use when splitting a label

SELECT [Label Column] AS [Label],
       10             AS [SPLIT LENGTH],
       '\r'           AS [LINE ENDING],
       ...
Group nodes into a cluster

SELECT [Cluster Id Column]         AS [CLUSTER],
       [Cluster Label Column]      AS [CLUSTER LABEL],
       [Cluster Style Name Column] AS [CLUSTER STYLE NAME],
       [Cluster Attributes]        AS [CLUSTER ATTRIBUTES],
       [Cluster Tooltip Column]    AS [CLUSTER TOOLTIP],
       [Node Id Column]            AS [Item],
       [Node Label Column]         AS [Label],
       [Node Style Name Column]    AS [Style Name],
       ...
Group nodes into two level clusters

SELECT [Outer Cluster Id Column]         AS [CLUSTER],
       [Outer Cluster Label Column]      AS [CLUSTER LABEL],
       [Outer Cluster Style Name Column] AS [CLUSTER STYLE NAME],
       [Outer Cluster Attributes]        AS [CLUSTER ATTRIBUTES],
       [Outer Cluster Tooltip Column]    AS [CLUSTER TOOLTIP],

       [Inner Cluster Id Column]         AS [SUBCLUSTER],
       [Inner Cluster Label Column]      AS [SUBCLUSTER LABEL],
       [Inner Cluster Style Name Column] AS [SUBCLUSTER STYLE NAME],
       [Inner Cluster Attributes]        AS [SUBCLUSTER ATTRIBUTES],
       [Inner Cluster Tooltip Column]    AS [SUBCLUSTER TOOLTIP],

       [Node Id Column]                  AS [Item],
       [Node Label Column]               AS [Label],
       [Node Style Name Column]          AS [Style Name],
       ...

Loop count substitution strings (useful in sortv= attributes, style names, and labels). 
  %clc% = Cluster count
  %scc% = Subcluster count
  %rsc% = Record set count

SELECT 'sortv=%clc%'    AS [CLUSTER ATTRIBUTES],
       'Border %clc% '  AS [CLUSTER STYLE NAME],
       'sortv=%scc%'    AS [SUBCLUSTER ATTRIBUTES],
       'sortv=%rsc%'    AS [Attributes],
       ...
FROM   [Worksheet Name$] 
WHERE  ...
ORDER BY [Outer Cluster Label Column] ASC,
         [Inner Cluster Label Column] ASC,
         [Node Label Column]          ASC
Show the graph immediately after all SQL statements processed
PREVIEW [ AS ( DIRECTED | UNDIRECTED ) GRAPH ]
Publish graphs as files
PUBLISH [ ALL VIEWS ] [ AS ( DIRECTED | UNDIRECTED ) GRAPH ] [ file prefix ]
Graph it, and display it in the workbook
PREVIEW AS UNDIRECTED GRAPH

buttons.screenTip: Describes the attributes used by various Graphviz tools
buttons.superTip: The table gives the name of the attribute, the graph components (node, edge, etc.) which use the attribute and the type of the attribute (strings representing legal values of that type). Where applicable, the table also gives a default value for the attribute, a minimum allowed setting for numeric attributes, and certain restrictions on the use of the attribute.
An on-line code editor for Graphviz written by Josh Hayes-Sheen for Computer Science students.
Online Graphviz editor with syntax higlighting.
Renders Graphviz diagrams as if sketched by hand.
GraphvizFiddle draws DOT language [directed] graphs. It is a dual-view Graphviz editor and playground for Graphviz written by Hermann Stamm-Wilbrandt.

buttons.screenTip: Renders Graphviz diagrams as ascii art.
buttons.superTip: Renders Graphviz diagrams as ascii art.
